summary:

This circumstance has led to substantial disruptions in global fuel supply chains and inland solutions across the United States. Due to recent fluctuations in fuel supply and increased ...

The post Mærsk announces $140 inland fuel surcharge in the US appeared first on The Loadstar.

title: Evri invests £4m in new Barnsley fulfillment center
summary:
Evri Group has announced a £4m (US$5.3m) investment in a new fulfillment center in Barnsley, UK, as part of its ongoing network expansion.

The 25,000m2 facility, located less than 100m from the company’s existing Barnsley hub, is scheduled to open in July 2026 following a fit-out phase that began after Evri took possession of the site on March 18.

The new center is designed to integrate directly with Evri’s largest operational hub, enabling parcels to enter the network on an hourly basis.

Continue reading Evri invests £4m in new Barnsley fulfillment center at Parcel and Postal Technology International.

title: DHL Global Forwarding expands Asia-Europe air freight capacity
summary:
DHL Global Forwarding has expanded its air freight capacity between Asia and Europe with the launch of new weekly services connecting key logistics hubs.

The company will operate Boeing 777F flights on routes linking Shanghai and Leipzig, as well as Hong Kong and Liège, aligned with the start of the summer aviation schedule. The services are supported by DHL Aviation assets and integrated with the DHL Express network.

According to DHL, the move is intended to strengthen capacity and reliability on the Asia-Europe corridor, where demand continues to grow.

Continue reading DHL Global Forwarding expands Asia-Europe air freight capacity at Parcel and Postal Technology International.

title: Drone strike keeps Salalah shut as disruption ripples across Gulf
summary:
Operations at Oman’s Port of Salalah remain suspended following an Iranian drone strike on Saturday that disabled a ship-to-shore crane, with APM Terminal’s hopes of a 48hr return to normalcy having been missed.
Minor injuries to a worker as a consequence of the strike was reported, with the port immediately evacuated, prompting Maersk’s terminal-operating division to announce the two-day suspension of operations.
Per the carrier’s advisory page, container operations at Salalah remained ...
